blocks.text: DTC P0102: Air Flow Meter Voltage Low

An air flow meter is installed in the air intake path to detect the amount of intake air. The built-in hot wire, whose temperature changes with increasing or decreasing current, is controlled to maintain a constant temperature. Therefore, as the intake air volume increases, the current flowing through the hot wire increases as heat is lost to the air. The intake air volume is calculated based on this current value, converted to voltage, and output to the PGM-FI ECU. If the air flow meter output voltage remains below a predetermined value for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows: Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: The output voltage of the air flow meter [air flow sensor] remains below 0.10V for 2.0 seconds or longer.

items.text: Ground fault in code (VGP line) between PGM-FI ECU and air flow meter
Disconnection of cord (VGP line) between PGM-FI ECU and air flow meter
Disconnection of air flow meter cord (power supply line)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
